Diatoms are single-celled algae encased in intricate glass-like shells called frustules. They produce roughly 20% of all oxygen on Earth and form the base of aquatic food chains. Diatom microscope microorganism

Paramecia are single-celled organisms covered in thousands of tiny hair-like cilia that propel them through water. They are among the most studied organisms in biology. Paramecium microscope microorganism

Known as "water bears," tardigrades are the most resilient animals known. They survive extreme radiation, the vacuum of space, and temperatures from −272°C to 150°C. Tardigrade microscope microorganism
